Artwork.title: Artwork
Artwork.description: Represents an artwork in the portfolio.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Artwork entity.

PortfolioSettings.title: PortfolioSettings
PortfolioSettings.description: Represents the portfolio settings for the website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PortfolioSettings entity.

contactInformation.description: Contact information for the portfolio owner.

definition.description: Stores artwork created by individual users. Access is restricted to the user who owns the artwork. Includes 'userId' parameter for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: artworkId
params.description: The unique identifier of the artwork.

definition.description: Stores global portfolio settings, such as 'about me' text and contact information. Access should be restricted to admin users, if applicable, otherwise any logged in user. The 'documentId' is used to ensure there's one PortfolioSettings document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document. Should be a constant value to ensure only one document exists.

firestore.reasoning: This Firestore data structure is designed for the DeepTerrorGG portfolio website, prioritizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging. It leverages path-based ownership and structural segregation to simplify security rules and eliminate the need for complex authorization logic. The structure uses dedicated collections for different data types, ensuring that each collection has a clear and consistent security posture. Specifically, `users/{userId}/artworks` establishes clear ownership, and `/portfolio_settings/{documentId}` allows admin control of the site's configuration. This segregation inherently supports the required QAPs, as `list` operations are naturally scoped and secure due to the absence of mixed data types within collections. Authorization Independence is achieved through path-based ownership; no `get()` calls are needed to determine access rights. Since all data is user-specific, there's no need to denormalize shared authorization data.
